# Typography System
<!-- nib-foundation: typography-system | generated: 2026-03-01 | do not edit manually -->

## Font Families

| Role | Token | Usage |
|------|-------|-------|
| Sans-serif | `fontFamily.sans` | All body text, UI labels, headings |
| Monospace | `fontFamily.mono` | Code blocks, technical strings, numeric data |

> The display font, if different from sans, is used only for hero headings. Most products use the sans font for display as well.

---

## Type Scale

The scale uses a **major-third ratio (1.25×)** by default, anchored at 16px (1rem).

| Step | Token | Size | Line Height | Weight | Use case |
|------|-------|------|-------------|--------|----------|
| caption | `fontSize.caption` | ~10px | 16px | 400 | Helper text, timestamps, metadata |
| label | `fontSize.label` | ~13px | 20px | 500 | Form labels, badges, table headers |
| body | `fontSize.body` | 16px | 24px | 400 | Paragraph text, default UI copy |
| body-lg | `fontSize.body-lg` | ~20px | 32px | 400 | Introductory paragraphs, pull quotes |
| heading-sm | `fontSize.heading-sm` | ~25px | 36px | 600 | Card titles, dialog headings |
| heading | `fontSize.heading` | ~31px | 48px | 600 | Section headings (H2) |
| heading-lg | `fontSize.heading-lg` | ~39px | 60px | 700 | Page headings (H1) |
| display | `fontSize.display` | ~49px | 72px | 700 | Feature headlines |
| display-lg | `fontSize.display-lg` | ~61px | 92px | 700 | Hero sections only |

> Exact values depend on the scale ratio configured during `nib brand init`.
> Run `nib brand build` and inspect `docs/design/system/tokens/typography.tokens.json` for resolved values.

**Scale ratios:**
- `major-third` (1.25) — balanced, works well for UI-dense products
- `perfect-fourth` (1.333) — more expressive, better for content-heavy sites

---

## Text Styles

Combine font size + weight + line height tokens into named text styles:

| Style | fontSize | fontWeight | lineHeight | Usage |
|-------|----------|------------|------------|-------|
| `display-lg` | display-lg | 700 | auto | Hero section single-line headlines |
| `display` | display | 700 | auto | Feature section headlines |
| `heading-lg` | heading-lg | 700 | auto | Page-level H1 |
| `heading` | heading | 600 | auto | Section H2 |
| `heading-sm` | heading-sm | 600 | auto | Card titles, H3 |
| `body-lg` | body-lg | 400 | auto | Lead paragraphs |
| `body` | body | 400 | auto | Default body text |
| `label` | label | 500 | auto | Form labels, table headers |
| `caption` | caption | 400 | auto | Helper text, metadata |

---

## Line Height Rules

Line heights follow a **4px grid** — every line height is a multiple of 4px.

Formula: `ceil(fontSize × 1.5)` snapped to nearest 4px.

- Tighter line heights (body and smaller) improve readability in dense UI
- Looser line heights (display) avoid collision when text wraps
- Never use `line-height: 1` in production — always use grid-snapped values

---

## Letter Spacing

| Scale step | Tracking | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------|
| display, heading | -0.01 to -0.02em | Slight negative tracking tightens large text |
| body, label | 0 (normal) | Default tracking for readability |
| caption, ALL CAPS | +0.05em | Slightly open tracking on small/caps text |

---

## Do / Don't

**Do:**
- Use `body` for all paragraph text
- Use `label` for form labels — it's 500 weight for contrast without bold
- Use `caption` for timestamps, file sizes, metadata
- Follow the 4px line height grid in custom components

**Don't:**
- Skew the type scale by adding intermediate sizes not in the token set
- Use `display-lg` for anything other than single-line hero text
- Set line heights in `em` or `%` — use the token values in `px`
- Set font-weight to anything other than 400, 500, 600, or 700

---
